channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Your AI Hit Its Limit. Your Knowledge Shouldn't.
item.description: Every AI conversation eventually resets. Claude runs out of messages. ChatGPT loses the thread after enough back-and-forth. Context windows fill up. It doesn't matter which model you use — at some...

item.title: Meta’s court losses could be just the beginning
item.description: Social media companies have long seemed impervious to legal threats. Meta, YouTube, Snap, and the rest have long waved off criticism of their platforms on free speech and Section 230 grounds. But...

item.title: Your AI Agent Crashed at Step 47. Now What?
item.description: Your agent is running a 50-step data pipeline. Extract, validate, transform, load. It's been working for 20 minutes. Step 47. OOM kill. Process gone. State gone. Now what? The State of Crash...

item.title: The AI Tried Again. This Time the Coffee Mug Actually Worked.
item.description: Last week I wrote about letting AI create a product and post it to LinkedIn. It failed spectacularly. The AI reused an old design, skipped artwork entirely, and posted a text-only merch promo to...

item.title: I built Customermates, a selfhostable, opensource Pipedrive CRM replacement
item.description: I built Customermates because I was unhappy with existing CRM tools. Pipedrive and HubSpot felt too hard to use, while the open-source alternatives I tried were missing important features I...

item.title: Why I Built an AI Companion
item.description: I’ve been a waiter, a construction worker, a DJ, a kitchen hand, a porter, a caregiver, and a supermarket cashier. I’ve washed dishes in Australia and meditated in the Himalayas. I’ve battled...

item.title: Residual Attention U-Net for Automated Multi-Class Segmentation of COVID-19Chest CT Images
item.description: {{ $json.postContent }}

item.title: I Let AI Create a Product and Post It to LinkedIn. It Failed Spectacularly.
item.description: Last night I ran an experiment: could an AI agent discover trending content, create a real product on my merch store, and post the promotion to LinkedIn — completely end-to-end? The answer was...

item.title: MCPKernel — The Missing Security Kernel for AI Agents
item.description: The Problem Nobody's Talking About Your AI agent — LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Copilot — calls tools autonomously. It reads files, executes code, makes HTTP requests. One prompt injection and your...

item.title: Why your word error rate (WER) benchmark might be lying to you
item.description: At AssemblyAI, we've spent years helping customers evaluate speech-to-text performance. Word Error Rate (WER) has long been the gold standard metric for this — and for good reason. It's simple,...

item.title: Why a Single Markdown File Can't Be Your AI Agent's Memory
item.description: A blunt reality check from the front lines of AI coding. On the Cursor forum, a developer asked why .cursorrules kept being ignored. The AI’s reply was painfully direct: "Even if you add Cursor...

item.title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather...

item.title: Sony is raising PS5 prices by $100 in April
item.description: Sony is raising the price of its PlayStation 5 consoles globally starting April 2nd. In an announcement on Friday, Sony says that the standard PS5 will now cost $649.99, up from $549.99....

item.title: AitM Phishing Targets TikTok Business Accounts Using Cloudflare Turnstile Evasion
item.description: Threat actors are using adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) phishing pages to seize control of TikTok for Business accounts in a new campaign, according to a report from Push Security. Business...

item.title: A New AI Documentary Puts CEOs in the Hot Seat—but Goes Too Easy on Them
item.description: The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist seeks the middle ground on a polarizing technology—and ends up letting tech execs like Sam Altman off the hook.

item.title: We Are At War
item.description: Rising geopolitical tensions are reflected (or in some cases preceded) by cyber operations, while technology itself has become politicized. Let’s admit it: we are in the middle of it....

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT 500 Questions. Here Are the Ads I Saw Most Often
item.description: Ads are rolling out across the US on ChatGPT’s free tier. I asked OpenAI's bot 500 questions to see what these ads were like and how they related to my prompts.

item.title: Bearlyfy Hits Russian Firms with Custom GenieLocker Ransomware
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian group called Bearlyfy has been attributed to more than 70 cyber attacks targeting Russian companies since it first surfaced in the threat landscape in January 2025, with recent...

item.title: Rank the 50 best Apple products
item.description: In honor of Apple’s 50th anniversary, we picked 50 of our favorite Apple products, from the OG iPhone to GarageBand. Now we’re ranking them as a community, and you can contribute here.

item.title: Here’s how to rank the 50 best Apple products ever
item.description: Apple turns 50 next week, and we're going to spend the week covering the company, its products, its legacy, and its future. But mostly, we're going to spend the week debating which Apple products...

item.title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
item.description: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part...

item.title: LangChain, LangGraph Flaws Expose Files, Secrets, Databases in Widely Used AI Frameworks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed three security vulnerabilities impacting LangChain and LangGraph that, if successfully exploited, could expose filesystem data, environment secrets, and...

item.title: Anthropic wins injunction against Trump administration over Defense Department saga
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that the Trump administration rescind recent restrictions it placed on the AI company.

item.title: Judge sides with Anthropic to temporarily block the Pentagon’s ban
item.description: After Anthropic's weeks-long standoff with the Pentagon, the company won one milestone: A judge granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction in its lawsuit, which sought to reverse its government...

item.title: Brendan Carr says his broadcast license threat wasn’t really about Iran war coverage
item.description: Federal Communications Commission Chair Brendan Carr never meant to threaten broadcast licenses over their coverage of the war in Iran, he told reporters after an event hosted by FGS and Semafor....

item.title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
item.description: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

item.title: David Sacks is no longer the White House AI and Crypto Czar
item.description: David Sacks, the venture capitalist and tech billionaire who'd become Silicon Valley's primary advocate inside the White House and a key architect of its aggressive AI policy initiatives, revealed...

item.title: Anthropic Supply-Chain-Risk Designation Halted by Judge
item.description: A judge temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s designation, clearing the way for Anthropic to keep doing business without the label starting next week.

item.title: Robot vacuums from Eufy and Roborock are over 50 percent for Amazon’s spring sale
item.description: A good robovac can save you a surprising amount of time. Luckily, Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is a great time to pick one up, as you can currently find deals on everything from basic, budget-friendly...

item.title: Apple’s Mac Pro is dead, apparently for good this time
item.description: The most expensive Mac you could get is no longer available on Apple's website, as 9to5Mac reports that the "cheese grater" Mac Pro workstation has been discontinued. It had been a part of the...
